content.problemStatement:
Athletes of Chefland have won GGG gold medals, SSS silver medals, and BBB bronze medals in the Paris Olympics.

Chef will be happy only if the Chefland team wins 555 medals of each type. Find the total number of additional medals the team needs to win to make Chef happy.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain three space-separated integers G,S,G, S,G,S, and BBB — the number of gold, silver, and bronze medals, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total number of additional medals the team needs to win to make Chef happy.
constraints: Number of test cases ≤ 10^5 (total input size fits in memory).
content.inputFormat: The input consists of space‑separated integers. It may contain one or more test cases. Each test case provides three integers G S B — the number of gold, silver and bronze medals already won. The file ends after the last test case (no explicit T is given).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line — the total number of additional medals required so that the team has at least 5 medals of each type.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Gold already equals 5 → need 0. Silver needs 5‑1 = 4. Bronze needs 5‑4 = 1. Total = 0+4+1 = 5.
Gold needs 5‑2 = 3, Silver needs 5‑1 = 4, Bronze needs 5‑1 = 4. Total = 3+4+4 = 11.
All medal counts already satisfy the requirement, so no additional medals are needed.

solution.approach: For each medal type we need at most 5 medals. If the current count is less than 5 we need (5 - count) more, otherwise we need 0 more. The answer for a test case is the sum of the three needed values: ans = max(0,5‑G) + max(0,5‑S) + max(0,5‑B). The algorithm processes each test case independently in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) where T is the number of test cases (constant work per case).
